note: janitf is a -tf agent noun with its own headword (MW janitṛ 'progenitor'); the lemma of a nominal form is the nominal stem janitf, not the bare root jan (jan belongs in root field only) — overruling 2-1 majority; 'родитель' accurately glosses the word itself

durDA is a transparent dus+DA formation lemmatized to itself (identity reduction, fine), but -DA derivatives are manner/distributive adverbs (dviDA, bahuDA) — so 'wrongly, in disarray' is adverbial; the noun gloss 'беспорядок' hits the semantic field but misrepresents the part of speech

kiranto = kirantaH, nom.pl. pres. ptc. of class-6 kirati < kF (ṝ, 'scatter, strew'); root kf (ṛ, 'do') would give kurvant-, never kirant- — the classic ṛ/ṝ length error; 'меча' (strewing) renders the true root's meaning correctly

sahArjunena parses either as saha + arjunena (a+a sandhi, adposition + instr.) or as instr. of bahuvrIhi sahArjuna- (saha- optionally retained, P. 6.3.82); both analyses reduce the token to stem sahArjuna and both mean 'together with Arjuna', so the reduction and gloss stand

viDunvAna is the class-5 middle pres. ptc. of vi+DU (Dunute -> Dunv-Ana); lemma viDu is the noun 'moon' — a nominal mis-tag betrayed by the empty root field; correct lemma viDU (root DU); 'размахивая' (brandishing) is exactly vi-DU with a weapon/bow

the token is the whole compound anarTa-trivarga, so its lemma is the full stem anarTatrivarga; keeping only the final member trivarga drops anarTa- (and trivarga alone conventionally means the dharma/artha/kAma triad) — overruling the majority; gloss covers the full compound but 'невыгода' undertranslates anarTa (вред, беда, зло)

same defect as id 76: lemma nATa keeps only the last member of the three-member tatpuruSa tridaSa-ISvara-nATa ('protector of the lords of the gods'); the token's lemma is the full stem tridaSeSvaranATa — overruling the majority; the gloss renders the entire compound accurately

the passive dIryate shows -Ir- < heavy F (cf. kF->kIryate, tF->tIryate, SF->SIryate): the root is dF (dṝ 'tear, split'), not df (dṛ 'heed', whose passive is driyate) — the ṛ/ṝ trap again, overruling the 2-1 majority; lemma should be anudF; 'разобщается' fits anu+dF passive

liNgin = liNga + possessive -in, a nominal with its own headword (MW liṅgin); reducing it to the marginal DhAtupATha verb liNg is the same nominal-vs-root category error as id 31 — lemma should be liNgin (or at most liNga); 'носитель знака' is the correct literal gloss
